replies.author: @FastLead308
replies.text:
For me its not nostalgia, I never stopped buying CD's, DVD's (a few Blue-Ray, but more now as the prices are often the same). I never stopped buying CD's as that is the highest quality you can get (beyond most human hearing, and everyone over ~25), converting them to MP3's takes about 5 minutes (limited by the speed a CD can spin) so I have CD's with my HiFi, plus every TV in the house has all the MP3's on USB drives directly connected.

FYI: Just about all TV's made in the last decade have media players built-in (for MP3, MP4 MKV, etc...) that read directly of USB or from networked storage (NAS/Plex). The USB thing is used in stores to play those video clips that run all day in shops.

I never liked the idea of "renting" my music and videos, being tied to an online service to keep what I paid for. Buy once and look after it and a CD/DVD will last multiple life times. That's also why I am able to buy so many second hand off Ebay.

I have also stopped buying PC games off Steam if the same product is on GOG. The same price but having a fully offline instalerl that I can backup, plus no DRM means I actually do own what I paid for. GOG does also provide updates and patches with their GOG-Galaxy application but I don't use that, I just download patches when and if I want them, plus being able to keep the previous versions install files helps if the game dev's screw up.
